creator: alane@geeksrus.net
text:
On Thu, Dec 13, 2001 at 09:39:13AM -0500, Vivek Khera wrote:
>>>>>> "AE" == Alan E <Alan> writes:
>
>AE> 	Is there a specific reason to need to build kdelibs2 without 
>AE> 	$X11BASE/bin in the $PATH?
>
>
>No, but my default root environment is without $X11BASE/bin for safety
>purposes (minimal environment for root.)  Occasionally some X ports
>fail, but those are usually only the ones that require imake.  This is

Some of the kde ports use imake. Kdebase2 does, and I'd guess offhand that
kdelibs2 does, too. And, as I said, there's also uil and moc(moc2)....

>the first port that seems to use the PATH to find the LIB PATH  to
>use, which is bizarre.  Anyhow, I'm not sure if just unconditionally

Xext is failing in a bad way. I do not believe it should.

>adding $X11BASE/bin to PATH would be the right thing to do in the X
>ports.

You're right... it wouldn't be a Good Idea.

>I'll just make a mental note to myself to adjust my path when building
>X ports.

You could also just make a little wrapper script for "make" that sets your
PATH to include $X11BASE/bin and then calls make itself. :)

Pete, we can close this one with a resolution of NOTABUG.

-- 
Alan Eldridge
Just another $LANGUAGE hacker.
